  | Huawei Services      |
  | Adventure Academy    |
  | Classic Reproduction |
</Info>


# Partner-managed
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization-partner-managed

In the Partner-managed authorization process, when a cardholder initiates a purchase, our system validates key criteria before sending the authorization request. These checks include verifying the card's validity, confirming the reserve balance, assessing spending limits, and applying merchant-specific rules. You will receive a webhook to either approve or decline the request based on your rules. Once approved, you should place a hold on the funds to ensure they are reserved for settlement, without any actual transfer of funds at this stage.

## Approving authorizations

When a cardholder initiates a purchase, our system validates several criteria before sending the authorization request. This process includes verifying the card's validity, checking the reserve balance, assessing spending limits, and applying custom merchant rules, such as merchant blocks. You will then receive a webhook to approve or decline the request. If approved, place a hold on the funds to reserve them for settlement. At this stage, no funds are transferred.

Once we've responded to the network, we'll send you a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook. If the transaction is declined, it will have a `status` of `declined`. You will need to persist this data for logging purposes.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($100)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Hold Placed
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Declining authorizations

<Warning>
  ### Limited availability

  Only Partner-managed programs can decline transactions. Rain-managed programs do not support this feature at this time.
</Warning>

To decline a cardholder authorization request, simply respond to the `transaction.requested` webhook event with an HTTP status code of `401` or any `40X` HTTP status code. You will then receive a `transaction.created` webhook with a status of "Declined" and amount of \$0.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($100)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Check balance ($100)
    Note over Your Ledger: Current funds ($50)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Transaction Declined
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook HTTP Code 401
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Declined
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($0)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

### Rejection codes

When declining a transaction, you can include a `rejectionCode` in your response body to indicate the reason for the decline.

| Code                     | Description                                         |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`     | Cardholder does not have sufficient funds           |
| `SUSPICIOUS_TRANSACTION` | Transaction appears suspicious or has elevated risk |

<CodeGroup>
  ```json Response Body theme={null}
  {
    "rejectionCode": "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  The `rejectionCode` field is optional. If you decline a transaction without providing a rejection code, Rain records it as a general webhook decline from your end.
</Info>

## Incremental authorizations

Some merchants, such as hotels and gas stations, may need to increase the initial authorization amount. This typically occurs when the final transaction amount is not known upfront. For example, hotels may add room service charges, or gas stations may adjust for the total fuel cost. Our system supports this through incremental authorizations, allowing merchants to request additional funds beyond the original authorization. Each increment undergoes the same validation process as the initial authorization.

We would notify you with a [`transaction.requested`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook followed by a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) and finally a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with the new total amount (original amount + increment amount)

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($50)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($50)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Place Hold ($50)
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($50)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Hold Placed
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($50)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,Your Ledger: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Increment Request (+$25)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($25)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Place Hold ($25)
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($75)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Hold Placed
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Increment Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($75)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Partial reversals

When a merchant needs to release part of an authorization amount, they can submit a partial reversal. We record the reversed amount, but the authorization hold remains unchanged until settlement to prevent fraud. This is common in scenarios like gas stations, where the initial hold may be higher than the final amount.

We'll first send you a send you a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with a `status` of `reversed`

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($100)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Hold Placed
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,Your Ledger: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Partial Reversal (-$40)
    Note over You,Your Ledger: Hold Maintained
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Reversal Processed
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($60)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Full reversals

A full reversal cancels an authorization entirely. This typically happens when a transaction is canceled or times out. Our system marks the authorization as reversed. As with partial reversals, the hold remains in place until settlement or until 30 days have passed.

We'll send you a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with a `status` of `reversed` and an `amount` of \$0.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook ($100)
    You->>+Your Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Your Ledger-->>-You: Hold Placed
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,Your Ledger: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Full Reversal
    Note over You,Your Ledger: Hold Maintained
    Note over Your Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Reversal Processed
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($0)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Partial authorizations

<Warning>
  ### Currently not supported

  Partial authorizations are not currently supported. When a cardholder has insufficient funds for the full requested amount, the transaction is declined.
</Warning>

Some merchants may request partial authorizations for the available balance when a cardholder has insufficient funds. This would allow a transaction to proceed with a reduced amount, which is common in scenarios such as pay-at-pump gas stations or retail transactions.

Rain does not currently support partial authorizations. If the cardholder's balance is insufficient for the full requested amount, the authorization is declined. You will receive a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook with a `status` of `declined`.

## Refund authorizations

Occasionally, merchants may send an authorization before processing a refund to a card. This resembles a standard authorization but with a negative amount. The key difference is that the user's ledger is not credited until the refund is fully settled.

We will send a [`transaction.requested`](/docs/transaction#requested) with a negative amount and upon your approval of this request (by responding with `200`) you will receive a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request (-$100)
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.requested` Webhook (-$100)
    Note over You,Your Ledger: No Credit Hold
	  You-->>-Rain: Webhook Approved
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook (-$100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Stale authorization closure

If an authorization remains pending and never settles, it becomes stale and is automatically closed. The closure period depends on the transaction type:

* **Refunds**: Closed after **31 days**
* **Low value positive transactions** (≤\$1000): Closed after **14 days**
* **High value positive transactions** (>\$1000): Closed after **31 days**

When a stale authorization is closed, any holds on the funds are released and the authorization is marked as closed.


# Rain-managed
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorization-rain-managed

In the Rain-managed Authorization process, our system ensures that each transaction undergoes a comprehensive validation before funds are held for settlement. These validations include verifying the card's validity, checking the user's balance, assessing spending limits, and enforcing custom merchant rules. Once a transaction is approved, we place a temporary hold on the funds, ensuring they are reserved for settlement. No funds are moved throughout this process. The authorization ensures that the requested amount remains available.

## Approving authorizations

When a cardholder initiates a purchase, our system checks several criteria to approve the transaction. This includes verifying card validity, checking the account balance, assessing spending limits, and applying custom rules such as merchant blocks. If the transaction is approved, we place a hold on the funds to ensure they are available for settlement. At this stage, no funds are transferred; the hold simply reserves the amount.

Once we've responded to the network, we'll send you a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook. If the transaction is declined, it will have a `status` of `declined`. You will need to persist this data for logging purposes.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
        'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
    'sequence': {
      'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
    }
  }
}%% 

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Check Balance
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Sufficient Balance
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Hold Placed
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Incremental authorizations

Some merchants, such as hotels and gas stations, may need to increase the initial authorization amount. This typically occurs when the final transaction amount is not known upfront. For example, hotels may add room service charges, or gas stations may adjust for the total fuel cost. Our system allows for incremental authorizations, enabling merchants to request additional funds beyond the original authorization. Each request for additional funds undergoes the same validation process as the initial authorization.

We'll notify you with a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook followed by a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with the new total amount (original amount + increment amount)

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($50)
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Check Balance
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Sufficient Balance
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Place Hold ($50)
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($50)
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Hold Placed
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($50)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,You: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Increment Request (+$25)
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Check Balance
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Sufficient Balance
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Increment Hold ($25)
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($75)
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Hold Placed
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Increment Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($75)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Partial reversals

When a merchant needs to reverse part of an authorization amount, they can submit a partial reversal. We record the reversed amount but do not reduce the authorization hold until settlement to prevent fraud. This is common in situations such as gas stations, where the initial hold may exceed the final transaction amount.

We'll first send you a send you a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with a `status` of `reversed`

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Check Balance
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Sufficient Balance
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Hold Placed
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,You: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Partial Reversal (-$40)
    Note over Rain,Our Ledger: Hold Maintained
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Reversal Processed
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($60)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Full reversals

A full reversal completely cancels an authorization, typically occurring when a transaction is canceled or times out. Our system marks the authorization as reversed, but the hold is not released until settlement or after 30 days.

We'll send you a [`transaction.updated`](/docs/transaction#updated) webhook with a `status` of `reversed` and an `amount` of \$0.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request ($100)
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Check Balance
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Sufficient Balance
    Rain->>+Our Ledger: Place Hold ($100)
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Our Ledger-->>-Rain: Hold Placed
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook ($100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
    Note over Merchant,You: Time passes
    Merchant->>+Rain: Full Reversal (-$100)
    Note over Rain,Our Ledger: Hold Maintained
    Note over Our Ledger: Total Hold ($100)
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Reversal Processed
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.updated` Webhook ($0)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Partial authorizations

<Warning>
  ### Currently not supported

  Partial authorizations are not currently supported. When a cardholder has insufficient funds for the full requested amount, the transaction is declined.
</Warning>

Some merchants may request partial authorizations for the available balance when a cardholder has insufficient funds. This would allow a transaction to proceed with a reduced amount, which is common in scenarios such as pay-at-pump gas stations or retail settings.

Rain does not currently support partial authorizations. If the cardholder's balance is insufficient for the full requested amount, the authorization is declined. You will receive a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook with a `status` of `declined`.

## Refund authorizations

Occasionally, merchants send an authorization before posting a refund to a card. This authorization appears similar to a standard authorization but with a negative amount. The key difference is that the user’s ledger is not credited until the refund has been settled.

You will receive a [`transaction.created`](/docs/transaction#created) webhook with a negative `amount` for the refund.

```mermaid theme={null}
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'base',
    'themeVariables': {
	    'textColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'primaryColor': '#212933',
      'primaryTextColor': '#FFFFFF',
      'primaryBorderColor': '#A6CFFF',
      'secondaryColor': '#F73196',
      'noteBkgColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteBorderColor': '#F4F3FF',
      'noteTextColor': '#6938EF'
    },
	  'sequence': {
	    'actorFontWeight': 'bold'
	  }
  }
}%%

sequenceDiagram
    Merchant->>+Rain: Auth Request (-$100)
    Note over Rain,Our Ledger: No Credit Hold
    Rain-->>-Merchant: Auth Approved
    Rain->>+You: `transaction.created` Webhook (-$100)
    You-->>-Rain: Webhook Acknowledged
```

## Stale authorization closure

If an authorization remains pending and never settles, it becomes stale and is automatically closed. The closure period depends on the transaction type:

* **Refunds**: Closed after **31 days**
* **Low value positive transactions** (≤\$1000): Closed after **14 days**
* **High value positive transactions** (>\$1000): Closed after **31 days**

When a stale authorization is closed, any holds on the funds are released and the authorization is marked as closed.


# Authorizing Transactions
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/authorizing-transactions



<Check>
  ### Partner-managed Programs only

  This page applies to Partner-managed Programs.
</Check>

To process transactions securely and efficiently, you must configure authorization settings and webhooks. These settings determine whether transactions are approved or declined, even if immediate responses are unavailable.

## Configuring default authorization settings

You can define default authorization settings in your [developer dashboard](/docs/developer-dashboard). These settings are used when:

* A webhook is not registered.
* A webhook request is not responded to in time.

Setting up defaults ensures that transactions continue to be processed according to your preferences, even when real-time decision-making is unavailable.

## Registering an authorization webhook

To gain real-time control over transaction approvals, register an [authorization webhook](/docs/transaction#spend) in your [developer dashboard](/docs/developer-dashboard). When a user initiates a transaction, Rain will send a webhook request containing:

* User details
* Card information
* Merchant details
* Transaction amount and currency

You can then approve or deny the transaction based on your internal rules.


# report
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/bin-reports

Webhook triggered when processor BIN report files arrive in storage, available for BIN-Sponsorship programs.

<Check>
  ### BIN-sponsorship program only

  This section applies to BIN-Sponsorship Programs. Take note of this information if you are using a BIN-Sponsorship Program.
</Check>

## `created`

The `created` webhook is triggered when the the processor's BIN report files have arrived in our storage servers, providing:

* The year, month and date of the files that have arrived.
* The format of the bin reports that have arrived from the processor

### Payload structure

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash bash theme={null}
  {
    resource: "report";
    action: "created";
    body: {
      id: uuid;
      year: number;
      month: number;
      day: number;
      format: "ssrp" | "csv" | "json"
    },
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

### Handling the webhook response

If your program supports responses, return:

* `200 OK` to approve the settlement.
* Any other status code to reject it.


# Blocked MCC's and Merchants
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/blocked-mccs-and-merchants



# What merchant categories are blocked by default?

Rain enforces restrictions on certain merchant categories by default. Transactions from these MCCs (Merchant Category Codes) are automatically declined:

| MCC  | Category                                                |
| ---- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| 2892 | Explosives                                              |
| 3484 | Small Arms and Weapons                                  |
| 4829 | Wire Transfers and Money Orders                         |
| 5122 | Drug Stores and Proprietary Drugs                       |
| 5681 | Furriers and Fur Shops                                  |
| 5932 | Antique Shops                                           |
| 5933 | Pawn Shops                                              |
| 5937 | Antique Reproductions                                   |
| 5960 | Direct Marketing – Insurance                            |
| 5962 | Direct Marketing – Travel Related Arrangement Services  |
| 5964 | Direct Marketing – Catalog Merchants                    |
| 5965 | Direct Marketing – Catalog and Retail                   |
| 5966 | Direct Marketing – Outbound Telemarketing               |
| 5967 | Direct Marketing – Inbound Telemarketing                |
| 5969 | Direct Marketing – Other                                |
| 5972 | Stamp and Coin Stores                                   |
| 5993 | Cigar Stores and Stands                                 |
| 6010 | Financial Institutions – Manual Cash Disbursements      |
| 6011 | Financial Institutions – Automated Cash Disbursements   |
| 6012 | Financial Institutions – Merchandise and Services       |
| 6050 | Quasi Cash – Member Financial Institutions              |
| 6051 | Quasi Cash                                              |
| 6211 | Securities Brokers and Dealers                          |
| 6529 | Remote Stored Value Load – Member Financial Institution |
| 6530 | Remote Stored Value Load – Merchant                     |
| 6531 | Payment Service Provider – Money Transfer               |
| 6532 | Payment Transactions – Member Financial Institution     |
| 6533 | Payment Transactions – Merchant                         |
| 6534 | Money Transfer – Member Financial Institution           |
| 6535 | Value Purchase – Member Financial Institution           |
| 6536 | MoneySend – Intracountry                                |
| 6537 | MoneySend – Intercountry                                |
| 6538 | MoneySend Funding Transaction                           |
| 6540 | Point-of-Interaction Funding Transactions               |
| 6555 | Mastercard Initiated Rebate/Rewards                     |
| 6611 | Overpayments                                            |
| 6760 | Savings Bonds                                           |
| 7272 | Escort Services                                         |
| 7273 | Dating or Escort Services                               |
| 7280 | Hospital Patient Personal Funds Withdrawals             |
| 7297 | Massage Parlors                                         |
| 7322 | Debt Collection Agencies                                |
| 7800 | Government-Owned Lotteries                              |
| 7801 | Government-Licensed Online Casinos                      |
| 7802 | Government-Licensed Horse/Dog Racing                    |
| 7948 | Race Organizations                                      |
| 7995 | Gambling, Betting, Lotteries                            |
| 8631 | Labor Unions                                            |
| 8651 | Political Organizations                                 |
| 8661 | Religious Organizations                                 |
| 9223 | Bail and Bond Payments                                  |
| 9401 | I-Purchasing Pilot                                      |
| 9405 | Intra-Government Purchases                              |
| 9411 | Government Loan Payments                                |
| 9700 | International Automated Referral Service (Visa only)    |
| 9701 | Visa Credential Server (Visa only)                      |
| 9702 | GCAS Emergency Services (Visa only)                     |
| 9721 | International Affairs (Embassies)                       |
| 9754 | Gambling – Horse Racing, Dog Racing, State Lotteries    |
| 9950 | Intra-Company Purchases                                 |

| Adventure Academy    |
| Classic Reproduction |


# Bulk Shipping
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/bulk-shipping

Bulk shipping allows you to group multiple cards into a single shipment for efficient delivery. This reduces shipping costs and simplifies distribution, especially for organizations managing large numbers of cards.

Even in bulk shipments, each card should be:

* Individually packaged.
* Labeled with its recipient mailing address.
* Ready for easy redistribution by the bulk shipment recipient.

<Warning>
  ### Shipping behavior

  Cards grouped in a bulk shipment must be individually addressed and packaged, so they can be forwarded or handed out without repackaging.
</Warning>

Follow the steps below to set up and manage bulk card shipments.

## Step 1: Create a shipping group

To initiate a bulk shipment, first create a shipping group using the [Create a bulk shipping group](/reference/shipping/create-a-bulk-shipping-group) endpoint.

This creates a unique `bulkShippingGroupId` that you will reference when creating cards to include in this shipment. Below, you can find an example request.

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash bash theme={null}
  curl --request POST \
       --url https://api-dev.raincards.xyz/v1/issuing/shipping-groups \
       --header 'accept: application/json' \
       --header 'content-type: application/json' \
       --data '
  {
    "address": {
      "line1": "123 Main Street",
      "city": "Springfield",
      "region": "Arizona",
      "postalCode": "123456",
      "countryCode": "US"
    },
    "recipientFirstName": "First",
    "recipientLastName": "Last",
    "recipientPhoneCountryCode": "1",
    "recipientPhoneNumber": "5555555555"
  }
  '
  ```
</CodeGroup>

The response includes:

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash bash theme={null}
  {
    "bulkShippingGroupId": "ABC123"
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

## Step 2: Create cards with the shipping group ID

When creating each card, include the `bulkShippingGroupId` in the request payload. All cards with the same ID will be grouped for shipment.

### Example workflow

1. Create a shipping group → Receive `bulkShippingGroupId = ABC123`
2. Create Card A with `bulkShippingGroupId = ABC123`
3. Create Card B with `bulkShippingGroupId = ABC123`

Both cards will be shipped together in one bulk package.

<Warning>
  ### Include shipping id when creating the card

  You must include the `bulkShippingGroupId` at the time of card creation. It cannot be added or changed later.
</Warning>

## Step 3: Understand grouping rules and cutoff times

Cards are grouped into the same shipment only if they:

* Share the same `bulkShippingGroupId`.
* Are created **before 12:00 PM EST**, Monday through Friday.

### Grouping timing example

| Card | Group ID | Created Time | Shipping Batch            |
| ---- | -------- | ------------ | ------------------------- |
| A    | XXX      | 11:30 AM EST | Batch 1 (together)        |
| B    | XXX      | 11:55 AM EST | Batch 1 (together)        |
| C    | XXX      | 12:15 PM EST | Batch 2 (separate)        |
| D    | YYY      | 11:45 AM EST | Separate from A, B, and C |

<Info>
  ### Plan card creation time

  To ensure cards are grouped correctly, plan card creation to complete before the 12:00 PM EST cutoff.
</Info>

## Common questions

### How many cards can I include in a bulk shipping group?

A minimum of two cards is required for a shipment to qualify as a bulk group.

### Can I change the shipping group after creating a card?

No. The `bulkShippingGroupId` must be set at the time of card creation and cannot be updated later.

### What happens if I forget to set up a shipping group?

Cards without a `bulkShippingGroupId` will be shipped individually, not as part of a bulk group.

### How are the cards packaged?

Each card is:

* Individually sealed
* Labeled with its specific recipient mailing address
* Easy to redistribute by the bulk shipment recipient

### Where can I find the shipping group ID?

The `bulkShippingGroupId` is returned in the response when you create a shipping group using the [Create a bulk shipping group](/reference/shipping/create-a-bulk-shipping-group) endpoint.


# card
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/card



## `updated`

Sent when a card status changes or when a user adds their card to a digital wallet. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash Consumer Program theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "card";
    action: "updated";
    body: {
      id: string; // cardId
      userId: string;
      type: "virtual" | "physical";
      status: "active" | "locked" | "canceled" | "notActivated";
      limit?: {
        amount: number;
        frequency: "per24HourPeriod" | "per7DayPeriod" | "per30DayPeriod" | "perYearPeriod" | "allTime" | "perAuthorization";
      };
      last4: string;
      expirationMonth: string;
      expirationYear: string;
      tokenWallets?: ("Apple" | "Google Pay")[];
      statusChangeReason?: string; // Reason for the status change (see Status change reasons)
    };
  }
  ```

  ```bash Corporate Program theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "card";
    action: "updated";
    body: {
      id: string; // cardId
      companyId: string;
      userId: string | null;
      type: "physical" | "virtual";
      status: "notActivated" | "active" | "locked" | "canceled" | "inactive";
      limit: {
        amount: number;
        frequency: "per24HourPeriod" | "per7DayPeriod" | "per30DayPeriod" | "perYearPeriod" | "allTime" | "perAuthorization";
      },
      last4: string;
      expirationMonth: string;
      expirationYear: string;
      tokenWallets: ["Apple"] | ["Google Pay"] | undefined;
    },
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  There are two different `id` fields. The one in the root is the `webhookId`, while the one on the `body` is the `cardId`. The `tokenWallets` field indicates whether the card has been added to `Apple` or `Google Pay`.

  **Consumer Programs** include a `userId` field. **Corporate Programs** include both a `companyId` field and an optional `userId` field (which may be `null`).
</Info>

### Fields

| Field                     | Type      | Description                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | --------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `id`                      | string    | Webhook ID                                                                                    |
| `resource`                | string    | Always `"card"`                                                                               |
| `action`                  | string    | Always `"updated"`                                                                            |
| `body.id`                 | string    | Rain card ID                                                                                  |
| `body.userId`             | string    | ID of the cardholder user                                                                     |
| `body.type`               | string    | `"virtual"` or `"physical"`                                                                   |
| `body.status`             | string    | New card status (see [Card statuses](#card-statuses))                                         |
| `body.limit`              | object    | Spending limit, if set (optional)                                                             |
| `body.limit.amount`       | number    | Limit amount in cents                                                                         |
| `body.limit.frequency`    | string    | Limit interval (see [Spending limit frequencies](#spending-limit-frequencies))                |
| `body.last4`              | string    | Last 4 digits of card number                                                                  |
| `body.expirationMonth`    | string    | Expiration month (no leading zero)                                                            |
| `body.expirationYear`     | string    | Expiration year                                                                               |
| `body.tokenWallets`       | string\[] | Digital wallet tokens (e.g., `"Apple"`, `"Google Pay"`), if applicable (optional)             |
| `body.statusChangeReason` | string    | Reason for the status change (see [Status change reasons](#status-change-reasons)) (optional) |

### Card statuses

| Status         | Description                                     |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `active`       | Card is active and can be used for transactions |
| `locked`       | Card is temporarily frozen                      |
| `canceled`     | Card is permanently deactivated                 |
| `notActivated` | Card has been created but not yet activated     |

### Spending limit frequencies

| Frequency          | Description     |
| ------------------ | --------------- |
| `per24HourPeriod`  | Daily           |
| `per7DayPeriod`    | Weekly          |
| `per30DayPeriod`   | Monthly         |
| `perYearPeriod`    | Yearly          |
| `allTime`          | Lifetime        |
| `perAuthorization` | Per transaction |

### Status change reasons

**Lock reasons:**

| Reason               | Description                                                      |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `api_lock_request`   | Card locked via the lock card endpoint                           |
| `pin_limit_exceeded` | Card automatically locked due to too many incorrect PIN attempts |

**Unlock reasons:**

| Reason                      | Description                                   |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `api_unlock_request`        | Card unlocked via the unlock card endpoint    |
| `last_4_api_unlock_request` | Card activated via the verify last 4 endpoint |

**Cancel reasons:**

| Reason                            | Description                                                           |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `api_deactivate_endpoint_request` | Card canceled via the deactivate card endpoint                        |
| `api_cancel_request`              | Card canceled via the update card endpoint (status set to `canceled`) |
| `api_update_cancel_request`       | Card canceled via the update card endpoint                            |
| `user_deleted`                    | Card canceled because the associated user was deleted                 |
| `team_member_deleted`             | Card canceled because the team member was removed                     |
| `card_replaced`                   | Card canceled because it was replaced with a new card                 |

**General:**

| Reason               | Description                                      |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `api_update_request` | Card status changed via the update card endpoint |

**Tokenization reasons:**

| Reason               | Description                                                |
| -------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `wallet_provisioned` | Card was added to a digital wallet (Apple Pay, Google Pay) |

### Trigger points

This webhook fires when:

* A card status changes due to API calls (lock, unlock, deactivate, update)
* A user or team member is deleted
* A card is replaced
* PIN limit is exceeded (from payment processor)
* A card is added to a digital wallet (includes `tokenWallets` and `statusChangeReason: "wallet_provisioned"`)

***

## `notification`

<Warning>
  ### Limited availability

  This webhook is currently triggered when:

  * A card provisioning request is declined or encounters an issue
  * Token wallet provisioning decisions are made
</Warning>

The `notification` webhook is sent when there are important status updates or decisions regarding card provisioning, particularly related to digital wallet token provisioning. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash Consumer Program theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "card";
    action: "notification";
    body: {
      id: string; // notificationId
      card: {
        id: string; // cardId
        userId: string;
      };
      tokenWallet: string;
      reasonCode: "PROVISIONING_DECLINED";
      decisionReason?: {
        code: DecisionReasonCode;
        description?: string;
      };
    };
  }
  ```

  ```bash Corporate Program theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "card";
    action: "notification";
    body: {
      id: string; // notificationId
      card: {
        id: string; // cardId
        companyId: string;
        userId: string | null;
      };
      tokenWallet: string;
      reasonCode: "PROVISIONING_DECLINED";
      decisionReason?: {
        code: DecisionReasonCode;
        description?: string;
      };
    };
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  There are multiple `id` fields: the root `id` is the `webhookId`, while `body.id` is the notification identifier, and `body.card.id` is the `cardId`. The `reasonCode` provides a specific code for the notification reason, while `decisionReason` contains additional context about any decisions made, particularly for provisioning scenarios.

  **Consumer Programs** include a `userId` field in `body.card`. **Corporate Programs** include both a `companyId` field and an optional `userId` field (which may be `null`) in `body.card`.
</Info>

### `reasonCode` values

Currently, only one value is supported:

* `"PROVISIONING_DECLINED"` - Indicates that digital wallet provisioning was declined

### `decisionReason.code` values

The `decisionReason.code` field can contain one of the following values:

* `"WALLET_PROVIDER_RISK_THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED"` - Wallet provider declined due to risk assessment
* `"DEVICE_SCORE_TOO_LOW"` - Device score below wallet provider's minimum threshold
* `"CVC_NOT_VALID"` - CVC/CVV code does not match the card
* `"EXCESSIVE_PROVISIONING_ATTEMPTS_ACROSS_ACCOUNTS"` - Too many provisioning attempts across accounts
* `"EXCESSIVE_CARDS_AND_PROVISIONING_ATTEMPTS"` - Too many cards and provisioning attempts
* `"EXPIRY_DATE_INVALID"` - Expiry date does not match the card
* `"UNKNOWN"` - Unknown or unspecified reason

The optional `decisionReason.description` field provides a human-readable explanation of the decision, particularly useful for understanding wallet provider-specific decline reasons.


# Card Management & Usage
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/card-management-usage



<Info>
  ### Are there any restrictions for using virtual cards on Facebook Ads?

  Generally, no. Virtual cards can be used on Facebook Ads. If a transaction is declined, review the decline reason in the webhook logs for more details.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### Are there restrictions for payments to international platforms?

  No special restrictions are enforced for international platforms. However, transactions may be declined if they trigger fraud rules or fall under blocked MCC categories.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### Can the card auto-upgrade to Visa Signature without credit requirements?

  No. Rain’s BIN assignment determines card tier (Platinum, Signature, Infinite).

  Upgrading requires a new card issuance and may involve a program change.

  Contact **[platform@rain.xyz](mailto:platform@rain.xyz)** for more information.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### Is the Platinum card included in the consumer contract at no cost?

  Yes. The Platinum card is provided at no additional cost to users. Startup and Enterprise programs may include partner-specific fees.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### What are the costs and requirements for the Visa Infinite card?

  Please contact **[platform@rain.xyz](mailto:platform@rain.xyz)** for pricing details.

  The Visa Infinite tier requires a minimum \$20,000 credit line to maintain status.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### What are the fraud rules for high-risk transactions?

  Fraud rules for high-risk transactions are based on:

  * MCC codes associated with high-risk categories
  * E-commerce merchant flags
  * General fraud detection rules

  Refer to the [High Risk MCC, High Risk E-Commerce, Fraud Rules](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/16XtLZlNpG29KoGSKB5JUUr09IvhA9rIl30I3r9m6T-Y/edit?usp=sharing) table for a full breakdown.
</Info>

<Info>
  ### What causes payment failures?

  Payment failures typically result from:

  * Fraud rule triggers
  * Incorrect card details
  * Spending limits being reached
  * Blocked merchant category codes (MCCs)

  For more, see [Decline Reasons](/docs/decline-reasons#/).
</Info>


# collateral
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/collateral

Webhook triggered when a new collateral contract is created for an approved user in a Rain-managed program.

<Check>
  ### Rain-managed tenants only

  We create a collateral contract for each approved user under the Rain-managed program.
</Check>

This webhook is triggered whenever a new collateral contract is created within the system.

<CodeGroup>
  ```typescript contractSampleWebhook theme={null}
  {
    id: string; // webhookId
    resource: "contract";
    action: "created";
    body: {
      id: string; // collateralContractId
      userAddress: string;
      chainId: number; 
      proxyAddress: string; 
      depositAddress: string; 
      controllerAddress: string;
      contractVersion: number;
    };
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>


# company
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/company



<Check>
  ### Corporate program only

  This section applies to Corporate Programs. Take note of this information if you are using a Corporate Program.
</Check>

## `updated`

<Warning>
  ### Compliance status update webhook

  This webhook is triggered whenever a company's compliance status is updated.
</Warning>

The `updated` webhook provides real-time updates on a company's compliance status, helping you track application progress and regulatory changes. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash bash theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "company";
    action: "updated";
    body: {
      id: string; // companyId
      name: string;
      address: undefined; // Currently hardcoded as undefined in the webhook
      applicationStatus: "approved" | "pending" | "needsInformation" | "needsVerification" | "manualReview" | "denied" | "locked" | "canceled";
      applicationExternalVerificationLink: {
        url: string; // e.g., "https://use-dev.rain.xyz/kyb"
        params: {
          userId: string; // companyId
          signature: string | undefined; // encrypted signature, undefined if status is approved
        }
      } | undefined; // undefined if status is approved
      applicationCompletionLink: {
        url: string; // e.g., "https://use-dev.rain.xyz/kyb" 
        params: {
          userId: string; // companyId
          signature: string | undefined; // encrypted signature, undefined if status is approved
        }
      } | undefined; // undefined if status is approved
      applicationReason: string; // comma-separated rejection labels, empty string if none
    }
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  There are two different `id` fields. The one in the root is the `webhookId`, while the one on the `body` is the `companyId`.
</Info>


# Compliance
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/compliance

Compliance processes ensure that user verification and regulatory requirements are met. This includes tracking application statuses, submitting necessary documentation, and integrating with compliance vendors.

The following sections provide details on key compliance topics:

<Columns>
  <Card title="Application states" href="/docs/application-states" />

  <Card title="KYC/KYB documents" href="/docs/compliance-documentation" />

  <Card title="Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO)" href="/docs/ultimate-beneficial-owner-ubo" />

  <Card title="Submitting pre-collected user documents" href="/docs/submitting-pre-collected-user-documents" />

  <Card title="Sumsub token sharing" href="/docs/sumsub-token-sharing" />

  <Card title="Persona token sharing" href="/docs/persona-token-sharing" />

  <Card title="Sharing data from external compliance vendors" href="/docs/sharing-data-from-external-compliance-vendors" />
</Columns>


# KYC/KYB Documents
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/compliance-documentation

When registering customers, you must submit some documents for the compliance checks. They differ depending on whether the customer is a person (KYC) or a business (KYB).

<Check>
  ### Endpoint options vs. accepted documents

  The documentation upload endpoints list multiple document types, but only those listed below are currently accepted for compliance. Other documents may be available for submission but will not be approved. These options exist in case compliance requirements change in the future.
</Check>

## KYC documents

For individuals, you'll need to submit:

* **Identification** (Passport, ID card, Driver's license, or Residence permit)
* **Selfie**

### Uploading Passport

When uploading passport, please leave the dropbox for `Front side` and `Back side` as empty. This dropdown selection only applies to `ID cards`, `Driver's license`, or `Residence permit` options.

## KYB documents

For business, you'll need to submit:

* **Certificate of incorporation**
* **Shareholder registry**

## Fastfail Checks

Automated checks run on uploaded documents to verify quality and completeness. If a check fails, the document is marked as inactive and the application moves to `needsVerification` status. The user must resubmit the document.

Fastfail checks verify the following:

* All pages and sides of the document are uploaded
* Document number and name fields are present and readable
* Document image is not a screenshot or cropped
* Document is not expired or issued in a forbidden territory
* Document image was not previously rejected
* Photo in the document contains a visible human face

### Error tags

Errors block the applicant from proceeding. A different document must be uploaded.

| Tag                         | Description                                                               |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `forbiddenDocument`         | Unsupported or unacceptable document type or country.                     |
| `differentDocTypeOrCountry` | Document type or country does not match what was specified or recognized. |
| `missingImportantInfo`      | Required document data is missing or unreadable.                          |
| `dataNotReadable`           | Data on the image is missing or unreadable.                               |
| `expiredDoc`                | Document is expired.                                                      |
| `documentWayTooMuchOutside` | Parts of the document are cropped.                                        |
| `noIdDocFacePhoto`          | Face is not clearly visible on the document.                              |
| `selfieFaceBadQuality`      | Face is not clearly visible on the selfie.                                |
| `screenRecapture`           | Image may be a photo of a screen or a screenshot.                         |
| `screenshot`                | Image is a screenshot.                                                    |
| `sameSides`                 | Same side of document uploaded as both front and back.                    |
| `shouldBeMrzDocument`       | Document should have an MRZ but none is readable.                         |
| `shouldBeDoubleSided`       | Both sides of the document must be provided.                              |
| `shouldBeDoublePaged`       | Full double-page photo of the document is required.                       |
| `documentDeclinedBefore`    | Same image was previously uploaded and declined.                          |
| `mrzNotReadable`            | MRZ code cannot be extracted due to poor quality or missing corners.      |
| `docExpiresSoon`            | Document expires within the minimum residual validity period.             |
| `missingDob`                | Document does not contain date of birth.                                  |
| `incompleteDob`             | Document does not contain the full date of birth (day, month, and year).  |

### Warning tags

Warnings allow applicants to proceed after retaking the document photo or providing missing information.

| Tag                      | Description                                           |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `badSelfie`              | Face or photo in the document is not clearly visible. |
| `dataReadability`        | Information in the document cannot be read.           |
| `inconsistentDocument`   | Uploaded photos do not belong to the same document.   |
| `maybeExpiredDoc`        | Document may be expired.                              |
| `documentTooMuchOutside` | Document is not fully in the frame.                   |


# Decline Reasons
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/decline-reasons



## Webhook Decline Messages Sent by Rain

For declined transactions, here is our list of all possible decline reasons that we send through the webhook:

| Decline Reasons                        |
| -------------------------------------- |
| invalid\_pin\_attempt\_limit\_exceeded |
| Account credit limit exceeded          |
| Card spending limit exceeded           |
| webhook\_declined                      |
| webhook\_timeout                       |
| card\_canceled                         |
| card\_locked                           |
| card\_not\_activated                   |
| blocked\_merchant                      |
| blocked\_mcc                           |
| blocked\_entity                        |
| balance\_inquiry\_not\_permitted       |
| transaction\_declined\_by\_rules       |
| transaction\_declined\_by\_risk\_rules |

Rain declines these transactions on clients' behalf:

| Reason               | Description                                                                                              |
| -------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Invalid PAN          | Transaction received from a PAN that wasn't issued by our system                                         |
| Wrong PIN            | Incorrect PIN entered                                                                                    |
| Invalid CVV          | CVV verification failed                                                                                  |
| Invalid ARQC         | Application Request Cryptogram validation failed                                                         |
| Invalid AAV          | Application Authentication Value validation failed                                                       |
| Card Expired         | Wrong expiry date or transaction received from an expired card                                           |
| Wrong ATC            | Application Transaction Counter mismatch                                                                 |
| Invalid Service Code | Service code doesn't match expected value                                                                |
| Non-ATM Withdrawal   | Withdrawal request other than from an ATM                                                                |
| Card Status          | The status of the card has been set to other than active (lost card, void, notActivated, etc.)           |
| Account Status       | The status of the account linked to the card is suspended or inactive                                    |
| Blacklisted Country  | Transaction from a blacklisted country                                                                   |
| Data Contradiction   | A data element defined in the document contradicts the txn request received                              |
| Card/Client Rules    | Rules defined for the card/client to decline for certain cases, like declining all e-commerce, ATMs etc. |
| Amount Limit         | Declining transactions exceeding a certain amount limit                                                  |
| Online PIN Limit     | Online PIN tries exceeded                                                                                |
| Offline PIN Limit    | Offline PIN tries exceeded                                                                               |
| PIN Change Mismatch  | PIN mismatch in a PIN change request                                                                     |
| AVS Failure          | Address Verification Service check failed                                                                |
| 3DS Failure          | 3DS verification failed                                                                                  |
| Cashback Unsupported | Cashback request received from a POS that doesn't support the cashback feature                           |
| E-commerce Flag      | Card flag set to decline all e-commerce transactions                                                     |
| ATM Cash Flag        | Card flag set to decline all ATM cash transactions                                                       |
| Balance Enquiry Flag | Card flag set to decline Balance enquiry over auth                                                       |


# Developer Dashboard
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/developer-dashboard

The Developer Dashboard allows you to manage your API keys, configure webhooks, and oversee your integration with Rain.

## Accessing your developer dashboard

We provide separate instances for **sandbox** and **production** environments:

* **Sandbox**: Initially, we create a sandbox account for you and send an invitation via email. You can access your developer dashboard from this account.
* **Production**: When you're ready to go live, you'll need a Rain Spend account.

[Sign up for a production account here](https://use.raincards.xyz/signup).

## Finding your API keys

Once your account is set up, navigate to the Config page to retrieve your API keys:

* [Sandbox link](https://use-dev.raincards.xyz/issuing/config)
* [Production link](https://use.raincards.xyz/issuing/config)

These keys are essential for authenticating API requests.

<Warning>
  ### Can't access the Issuing page?

  If you don't see the page, reach out to us at [platform@rain.xyz](mailto:platform@rain.xyz) for assistance.
</Warning>


# disputes
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/disputes



Rain sends webhook notifications when disputes are created or updated. These webhooks work the same way for both consumer and corporate programs.

## `created`

<Warning>
  ### Dispute created webhook

  This webhook is sent when a new dispute is created for a transaction.
</Warning>

This webhook is sent immediately after a dispute is successfully created. Only one active dispute (pending or in-review) is allowed per transaction. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```javascript webhook.js theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "dispute";
    action: "created";
    version: "1.1.0";
    body: {
      id: string; // disputeId
      transactionId: string;
      status: "pending";
      disputeType: "fraud" | "creditNotProcessed" | "serviceNotReceived" | "merchandiseIssue" | "other";
      textEvidence?: string;
      disputeAmount: number; // disputed amount in cents
      creditAmount?: number;
      createdAt: string; // ISO-8601 timestamp
    };
    eventReceivedAt?: string; // ISO-8601 timestamp
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  There are two different `id` fields. The one in the root is the `webhookId`, while the one in the `body` is the `disputeId`. The `disputeType` field indicates the category of the dispute. The `textEvidence` field is optional and may not be present in all dispute webhooks. The `disputeAmount` field contains the disputed amount in cents and must be less than or equal to the transaction amount. When creating a dispute, `disputeAmount` is optional—if omitted, it defaults to the full transaction amount. This allows partial disputes when you only want to dispute a portion of the total. The `creditAmount` field is optional and only present when a dispute is accepted, storing the credit amount in cents.
</Info>

## `updated`

<Warning>
  ### Dispute status update webhook

  This webhook is sent when an existing dispute is updated.
</Warning>

This webhook is sent whenever a dispute status changes or when text evidence is updated. This includes status changes to "inReview", "accepted", "rejected", "canceled", and "resolvedByMerchant", as well as updates to text evidence. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```javascript webhook.js theme={null}
  {
    id: string;  // webhookId
    resource: "dispute";
    action: "updated";
    version: "1.1.0";
    body: {
      id: string; // disputeId
      transactionId: string;
      status: "pending" | "inReview" | "accepted" | "rejected" | "canceled" | "resolvedByMerchant";
      disputeType: "fraud" | "creditNotProcessed" | "serviceNotReceived" | "merchandiseIssue" | "other";
      textEvidence?: string;
      disputeAmount: number; // disputed amount in cents
      creditAmount?: number;
      createdAt: string; // ISO-8601 timestamp
      resolvedAt?: string; // ISO-8601 timestamp, present for resolved disputes
    };
    eventReceivedAt?: string; // ISO-8601 timestamp
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  There are two different `id` fields. The one in the root is the `webhookId`, while the one in the `body` is the `disputeId`. The `disputeType` field indicates the category of the dispute. The `textEvidence` field is optional and may not be present in all dispute webhooks. The `disputeAmount` field contains the disputed amount in cents and must be less than or equal to the transaction amount. When creating a dispute, `disputeAmount` is optional—if omitted, it defaults to the full transaction amount. This allows partial disputes when you only want to dispute a portion of the total. The `creditAmount` field is optional and only present when a dispute is accepted, storing the credit amount in cents. The `resolvedAt` field only appears when the dispute is resolved (status is "canceled", "accepted", "rejected", or "resolvedByMerchant").
</Info>

### Status values

The `status` field can be one of the following:

* `"pending"` - Dispute has been created and is awaiting review
* `"inReview"` - Dispute is being reviewed
* `"accepted"` - Dispute was accepted
* `"rejected"` - Dispute was rejected
* `"canceled"` - Dispute was canceled
* `"resolvedByMerchant"` - Dispute was resolved because the merchant issued a direct refund

## Automatic Resolution from Merchant Refunds

When a merchant issues a refund on a transaction with an active dispute, Rain automatically resolves the dispute with status `"resolvedByMerchant"`. This occurs when:

1. A cardholder files a dispute on a transaction (status: `"pending"` or `"inReview"`)
2. The merchant independently processes a refund for that transaction
3. Rain detects the merchant refund settlement

When Rain detects the refund:

* The dispute status changes to `"resolvedByMerchant"`
* The `creditAmount` is set to the refund amount
* The `resolvedAt` timestamp is populated
* You receive a `dispute.updated` webhook with the new status
* The cardholder account is credited with the refund amount

This status is distinct from other terminal states:

* `"accepted"` - Dispute was won through the card network dispute process
* `"rejected"` - Dispute was denied through the card network dispute process
* `"canceled"` - Dispute was withdrawn or invalidated
* `"resolvedByMerchant"` - Dispute was automatically resolved because the merchant issued a direct refund

<Info>
  When a dispute is resolved by merchant refund, you do not receive a `dispute.chargebackCreated` webhook because the credit comes from the merchant refund settlement, not from a chargeback process.
</Info>

## `chargebackCreated`

<Warning>
  ### Dispute chargeback created webhook

  This webhook is sent when a dispute chargeback is successfully processed.
</Warning>

This webhook is sent after a dispute is accepted and the reimbursement is processed. It allows you to directly link the chargeback transaction back to the original dispute without having to match amounts and timing. This webhook is informational only and does not require a response.

<CodeGroup>
  ```json webhook.json theme={null}
  {
    "resource": "dispute",
    "action": "chargebackCreated",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "body": {
      "id": "dis_9z8y7x6w5v4u",
      "disputeId": "dis_9z8y7x6w5v4u",
      "transactionId": "txn_3m2n1o0p9q8r",
      "originalTransactionId": "txn_4k3j2h1g0f9e",
      "amount": -5000,
      "originalTransactionAmount": 10000
    }
  }
  ```
</CodeGroup>

<Info>
  ### Key fields

  * `id` and `disputeId` - Both contain the dispute ID. The root `id` is used for webhook indexing.
  * `transactionId` - The ID of the new chargeback transaction created for the reimbursement.
  * `originalTransactionId` - The ID of the original disputed transaction.
  * `amount` - The chargeback amount in cents. This value is always negative (credit to the account). For example, `-5000` represents a \$50.00 chargeback.
  * `originalTransactionAmount` - The original transaction amount in cents.
</Info>

### When is this webhook sent?

The `dispute.chargebackCreated` webhook is sent after:

1. A dispute is accepted (status changes to `"accepted"`)
2. The reimbursement transaction is created
3. The account is credited

You will also receive a `transaction.completed` webhook for the chargeback transaction. The `dispute.chargebackCreated` webhook provides the explicit link between the chargeback and the dispute.

## Dispute Reimbursement

After a dispute is accepted (`status` changes to `"accepted"`), Rain automatically processes the reimbursement and credits the account.

When the reimbursement is processed, you will receive two webhooks:

1. **`dispute.chargebackCreated`** - Links the chargeback transaction directly to the dispute (see above)
2. **`transaction.completed`** - Standard transaction webhook for the chargeback transaction

The chargeback transaction has:

* Negative transaction amount (credit to the account)
* Amount matches the `creditAmount` from the dispute
* Transaction appears in your transaction history
* Account balance updated to reflect the credit

### Identifying reimbursement transactions

Reimbursement transactions can be identified by:

* The `dispute.chargebackCreated` webhook, which provides the `transactionId` linked to the `disputeId`
* Negative transaction amount (credit)
* Transaction type of `"spend"`

<Tip>
  Use the `dispute.chargebackCreated` webhook for the most reliable way to link chargeback transactions back to disputes.
</Tip>


# Escalations and Support Paths
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/escalations-and-support-paths

Use this guide to determine when to escalate issues to Rain Support, how to escalate and where to find reference codes for troubleshooting transactions.

<Info>
  ### How do I escalate an issue to Rain Support?

  Use Slack and tag **@RainSupport** directly.

  To help the team triage faster, include:

  * **Transaction ID**
  * **Last 4 digits of the card**
  * **User ID** (if applicable)
  * Any available evidence or error logs
</Info>

<Info>
  ### When should I escalate an issue to Rain Support?

  You should escalate when:

  * A refund has been pending for more than 30 business days.
  * A KYC application is stuck for more than 3 business days.
  * A user reports an unauthorized transaction.
  * You want to block a suspicious merchant.
</Info>


# Overview
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/faq-pre-go-live

KYC compliance requirements for individual and corporate applications, including required documents and information.

## Compliance Requirements

To meet compliance standards, the following information is required for both individuals and companies.

## Creating a Corporate Application for a Company

### Individual Required Information:

* First Name
* Last Name
* Birth Date
* National ID (SSN for US persons)
* Country of Issue
* Email
* Physical Address
* IP Address

### Optional Information:

* Role
* Wallet Address

### Company Information Required:

1. Company Name
2. Company Physical Address (Street, City, Post Code, Country Code)
3. Entity Object
4. Legal Name
5. Type (LLC, INC.)
6. Description of Activities
7. TaxID
8. Website
9. Expected Monthly Spend

### Ultimate Beneficial Owners:

1. Unique Identifier
2. First Name
3. Last Name
4. Birth Date
5. National ID
6. Country of Issue
7. Email
8. Address (Street, City, Post Code, Country Code)

### Documents:

1. Corporate Documents:

   * Certificate of Incorporation
   * Company Registration
   * Shareholder/Member register

2. UBO ID documents:

   * Passport
   * Driver's license
   * National ID card

## Create a Consumer Application for an Individual

### Required Information:

* ID String
* First Name
* Last Name
* Birth Date
* National ID Number (SSN for US persons)
* Country of Issue
* Email
* Physical Address
* Country Code + Phone Number
* IP Address
* Iovation Blackbox
* Occupation
* Salary (can be range)
* Account Purpose
* Expected Monthly Volumes (can be a range)

### Optional Information:

* Wallet Address
* Chain ID
* Contract Address
* Verification Documents already collected

## Next Steps (Compliance)

Once the **Customer Compliance Uploads** section in Rocketlane is completed, you will receive the **user agreements** shortly thereafter.

## Corporate Structure vs Org Chart

**Corporate Structure:** Outlines all the entities within your corporation (e.g., subsidiaries, parent companies).

**Organizational Chart:** Visualizes how employees are structured and their reporting relationships within the organization.

## API Documentation Access

Our API documentation is password-protected.

* If you need access, tag @RainSupport in Slack.
* Once you receive the password, store it securely for future reference.

## Handling Your Own Compliance Program Audit

### Post-Invoice Steps:

1. Once the invoice for your compliance program is processed, a kickoff call will be scheduled.
2. Additional documentation may be required from your team to share with Rain's compliance auditor.

### Audit Process:

1. The audit typically takes \~5 weeks to complete.
2. "No news is good news" during this time.
3. Upon completion, our compliance team will schedule a call to discuss findings and outline next steps.

## Custom Cards

If you are interested in custom cards, follow these steps:

### Contact Support:

1. Tag @RainSupport in Slack.
2. Specify the type of custom card you are interested in (e.g., physical or virtual).

<Note> To review card requirements, refer to the [Issuing Cards](/docs/issuing-cards) page. </Note>


# FAQs: Post-Go Live
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/faqs-post-go-live

This page will help you with Rain once you're live.

## Customer Support (Post Go-Live)

**Tag @RainSupport in Slack message to have a ticket created and ensure your question gets a response.**

## Adding Cards to Mobile Wallet (Apple Pay + Google Pay)

If a user encounters issues adding a card to their mobile wallet, advise them to:

1. Attempt to add a different card to confirm if the issue is wallet-specific.

2. Common reasons for failure:

   * The phone originates from a different country than the card.
   * The phone is flagged as high risk by Apple or Google.
   * The user has attempted to add multiple cards recently (rate limits apply).

**Resolution:** Suggest the user wait 24 hours and try again, as only one card can be added within a 24-hour period.

## Adding Card to Merchant

For users unable to add their card to a merchant, suggest:

* Using a Puerto Rico zip code during the purchase process.
* If the issue persists, tag @RainSupport in Slack and provide the transaction ID.

## Crediting Refunds

Refunds may take up to 30 business days after issuance by the merchant to reflect in the user's account.

* If 30 days have passed, escalate by tagging @RainSupport in Slack with the transaction ID.

## Adding Admin to SmartContract Wallet

To add an admin:

1. Use a blockchain explorer like Etherscan.
2. Log in with the owner wallet.
3. Call the `addAdmin` function.

## Reversed Transactions

Reversed transactions can take up to 30 days to appear in the user's account.

* If it has been over 30 days, tag @RainSupport in Slack with the transaction ID.

## Changing Card Shipping Address

We **cannot modify** card shipping addresses once submitted. However:

1. Tag @RainSupport in Slack to cancel the user's current card.
2. Inform the user to request a new card with the correct shipping address.

## Handling Card Declines

When a card is declined:

1. Check the **declinedReason** in the webhook response.
   * Popular decline reasons:
     * Blocked MCC, not enough spending power, volume limit (gaming, AFD), CVV mismatch
2. Verify if the transaction falls under blocked MCCs (Merchant Category Codes).
3. Inform the user of the decline reason.

## Blocking Merchants

To block a suspicious merchant:

1. Tag @RainSupport in Slack.
2. Provide the **reason** for blocking the merchant.
3. The team will take action to block the merchant.

## Fraud Rules High Risk

* High Risk MCC, High Risk E-Commerce, Fraud Rules
  * [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/16XtLZlNpG29KoGSKB5JUUr09IvhA9rIl30I3r9m6T-Y/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/16XtLZlNpG29KoGSKB5JUUr09IvhA9rIl30I3r9m6T-Y/edit?usp=sharing)

## Pending Status For Merchant Refunds

When a status remains pending but the merchant has issued a refund:

* If the transaction is pending for over 10 days, it is likely to fail. We typically wait before clearing the authorization from the balance.
* Refunds can take an additional **30 business days** after the 10-day period to reflect.

## Application in Pending State

If an application remains pending, check the following:

1. Verify the quality of the submitted documents (e.g., ensure the **front of the ID card** and **selfie** are labeled correctly in the API).
2. Wait **3 business days** after submission. If unresolved, tag @RainSupport in Slack with the **User ID**.

## User Going Through Additional Verification

For users required to complete additional verification:

* Ensure documents are high quality and properly labeled (e.g., front of ID labeled correctly, selfie labeled appropriately in the API).
* The user will be prompted to complete verification. No further action is required from your side.


# First Steps
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/first-steps

Before you start using the guides, it is important to identify which program type and management model applies to your implementation. This will ensure you follow the correct guides and API references.

## Step 1: Identify your program type

Rain offers two types of card programs. Your company will be using one of these:

| Card Program          | Description                                                                                               |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Consumer Program**  | Your company issues cards directly to individuals (e.g., customers signing up for a branded credit card). |
| **Corporate Program** | Your company issues cards to businesses or employees for expense management or corporate spending.        |

Your internal team should have provided guidance on which program(s) your company is using. If unsure, check with your account manager before proceeding.

## Step 2: Determine your management model

Each program type can operate in one of two management models:

| Model               | Description                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Rain-Managed**    | Rain handles certain operational aspects, including smart contract infrastructure. If your company is using Rain’s smart contracts for collateralized credit limits, you are in this model.            |
| **Partner-managed** | Previously known as Self-Managed. Your company manages its own funds and operations. If your company handles collateral and funds independently without Rain's smart contracts, you are in this model. |

<Info>
  ### Requirement: Wallet Addresses for Rain-Managed Clients

  Rain-Managed clients are required to have wallet addresses for each of their users. If your users don't have wallet addresses, you can use external wallet providers such as Turnkey, Zerodev, Dynamic, Privy, or similar services to provision them.
</Info>

## Step 3: Find the right guides

Once you have determined your program type and management model, you can follow the appropriate documentation. Each guide in this portal specifies if there are any program types it does not apply to, so you can navigate directly to what’s relevant to your implementation.

On guides that are not relevant for all types of programs, you'll see a callout like the one below:

<Check>
  ### \[Example] Consumer program only

  This section applies to Consumer Programs. Take note of this information if you are using a Consumer Program.
</Check>

If you are unsure about your program setup, check with your internal team or reach out to your Rain account manager.

If you already know the type of program you're using, we recommend that you start by learning about the [Basic Concepts](/docs/developer-dashboard).


# Force Capture
Source: https://docs.rain.xyz/docs/force-capture



## Transaction Completed

A force capture occurs when a merchant settles a transaction without prior authorization. These transactions bypass the authorization phase and proceed directly to settlement. This flow is typically used in offline scenarios, such as in-flight purchases where the merchant does not have internet access.

<CodeGroup>
  ```bash bash theme={null}
  Headers
  {
    "Signature": "b0660d7918f5ac372cfea484dd42646df38909aff494b5308aa9b9cf2cf855d6",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
  }
  Body
  {
    "id": "3394c9f0-af89-437d-afdb-c7cf3cabe245",
    "body": {
      "id": "feaec7b0-a9d5-4907-b05f-887661a54cd7",
  ```
